sources.name: Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities
sources.dataset: Council tax levels set by local authorities

sources.name: HM Treasury / DLUHC
sources.dataset: Local authority revenue expenditure and financing

sources.name: Institute for Fiscal Studies
sources.dataset: Local government funding: the picture in 2024

metadata.methodology: Band D council tax figures are the average charge across all English billing authorities for a Band D property, published annually by DLUHC. Government grant figures are net revenue support grant and other formula grants in real terms (2024 prices, GDP deflator). Figures exclude COVID emergency grants.
knownIssues: Council tax averages mask very large variation between local authorities; some households pay over £3,000 per year while others pay under £1,200 for the same band
Government grants to local authorities have multiple components that have changed over time; comparing like with like requires adjusting for grant reclassifications
Real-terms calculations use the GDP deflator; using CPI or RPIJ would give somewhat different figures for the scale of cuts
